disease.name: Alcoholic Hepatitis
disease.note: Acute inflammatory liver syndrome occurring in patients with heavy alcohol use. Severity assessed by Maddrey's Discriminant Function (mDF) and Lille score. High short-term mortality in severe disease (30-50% at 28 days).

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Severe AH (DF >=32) -> URGENT steroid/Lille evaluation; Mild-moderate (DF <32) -> SUPPORTIVE care; Very severe (DF >60, MELD >30) -> ICU

historyFeatures.note: Typically >40g/day women, >60g/day men for >10 years; recent binge common
Variceal bleed or portal hypertensive gastropathy

laboratory.name: AST and ALT
laboratory.indication: AST > ALT (ratio typically 2:1), neither >500 IU/L; hallmark pattern

laboratory.name: GGT
laboratory.indication: markedly elevated, disproportionate to ALT; GGT/ALP ratio >1.5

laboratory.name: Total and direct bilirubin
laboratory.indication: elevated, used in mDF and MELD scores

laboratory.name: INR/PT
laboratory.indication: prolonged in severe disease; used in mDF = 4.6 x (PT_patient - PT_control) + bilirubin

laboratory.name: Serum sodium
laboratory.indication: prognostic; hyponatremia associated with poor outcomes

laboratory.name: Maddrey Discriminant Function (mDF)
laboratory.indication: 4.6 x (PT_patient - PT_control) + serum bilirubin mg/dL; >=32 defines severe AH

laboratory.name: MELD score
laboratory.indication: prognostic; MELD >20 associated with high mortality

laboratory.name: Lille score
laboratory.indication: calculated at day 7 of steroids; <0.45 = responder continue steroids; >0.45 = non-responder stop steroids

laboratory.name: Ascitic fluid analysis (if ascites present)
laboratory.indication: SBP diagnosis; PMN count, culture, albumin for SAAG

imaging.name: Abdominal ultrasound with Doppler
imaging.indication: liver morphology, ascites, hepatic vein patency (rule out Budd-Chiari)

imaging.name: CT abdomen (if clinical uncertainty)
imaging.indication: assess for other causes; hepatomegaly with fatty infiltration; cirrhosis if chronic

management.protocolReference: AASLD Practice Guidelines: Alcoholic Liver Disease; EASL Clinical Practice Guidelines: Alcohol-Related Liver Disease

pathways.criteria: Maddrey's DF <32, no encephalopathy, stable INR/bilirubin, no organ failure
pathways.treatment: Supportive care. Abstinence from alcohol (counseling, medical management of alcohol withdrawal). Nutritional support: 1.2-1.5g/kg protein, 25-35 kcal/kg/day. Thiamine 100mg IV daily x 5 days + folic acid 1mg PO daily. Multivitamin supplementation. Monitor LFTs, INR, bilirubin, creatinine. No corticosteroid therapy (no benefit if mDF <32).

pathways.criteria: Maddrey's DF >=32, no contraindications to steroids, no active infection
pathways.treatment: Rule out infection (blood cultures, ascitic fluid, chest X-ray, urine). Methylprednisolone 32mg IV daily OR prednisolone 40mg PO daily x 28 days + taper. Calculate Lille score at day 7. If Lille <0.45 (responder): complete 28-day course. If Lille >0.45 (non-responder): STOP steroids (no benefit; increased infection risk). Alternative to steroids: pentoxifylline 400mg PO TID (weaker evidence; never first-line). Nutritional support (enteral preferred if unable to eat). Manage alcohol withdrawal with benzodiazepines (monitor for encephalopathy confusion). Monitor for infection during steroid therapy.

pathways.criteria: Maddrey's DF >=60, MELD >30, or multiple organ failure
pathways.treatment: ICU admission. Corticosteroids as above if no contraindication. Evaluate Lille score at day 7. Consider N-acetylcysteine (NAC) 150mg/kg IV load + 50mg/kg over 4h + 100mg/kg over 16h — may reduce 28-day mortality. Supportive intensive care: renal replacement therapy if needed; vasopressors for shock; mechanical ventilation for airway protection. Early transplant evaluation: selected patients with no contraindications (good psychosocial support, first episode, commitment to abstinence) may be candidates for early liver transplantation. Abstinence counseling mandatory.

pathways.criteria: Lille score >0.45 at day 7 on steroids
pathways.treatment: STOP corticosteroids immediately. No benefit from continuing; risk of infection increases. No alternative pharmacotherapy proven effective (pentoxifylline not recommended as salvage). Supportive care + nutritional support + abstinence. Early transplant evaluation if candidate. Manage complications (ascites, encephalopathy, renal failure, infection). Consider palliative approach if not transplant candidate and progressive multiorgan failure.

disease.emergencyPriority: high if DF >=32; medium if DF <32
